headline: Exxon Mobil’s Next Big Milestone Could Be $197
summary: Exxon Mobil (NYSE:XOM) trades fairly priced at current levels. The 24/7 Wall St. price target points to $141.98 over the next 12 months, modestly below the current quote of $152.75. Our recommendation is hold, with a confidence level of 90%. The longer-dated bull case, however, points to $197.38 by 2031, driven by LNG and Guyana ... headline: USO and BNO Have Soared 100% Plus in 2026, Here’s the Better Pick for Next
summary: Crude oil cleared $100 a barrel multiple times in April 2026, and the move was not subtle. WTI peaked at nearly $115 in early April; Brent reached about $138 in the same period; and the three ETFs most investors reach for to trade crude have moved with the underlying. headline: Exxon’s Earnings Beat, Buybacks, and Governance Fight Might Change The Case For Investing In Exxon Mobil (XOM)
summary: In recent weeks, Exxon Mobil reported first-quarter 2026 earnings that exceeded many analyst expectations, continued its large share repurchase program, reaffirmed a US$1.03 quarterly dividend, and faced fresh shareholder activism over its plan to redomicile from New Jersey to Texas and the design of its new retail voting program. The New York City Police Pension Fund’s call to expand retail voting choices and oppose the Texas move highlights growing investor focus on shareholder rights...
